Added a builtins-lib bootstrap guard test covering String.fromCharCode, String.prototype.constructor, String.prototype.trim, String.prototype[Symbol.iterator], String Iterator next, and String Iterator @@toStringTag. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-builtins --lib; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests; cargo fmt --all --check; git diff --check; cargo clippy -p lyng-js-builtins --lib --all-features -- -W clippy::pedantic (exits 0 with the existing 555-warning backlog).

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Moved RegExp constructor and RegExp.prototype descriptor installation out of public.rs into public/families/regexp.rs. The RegExp family now owns its descriptor atoms, species accessor, prototype symbol methods, and flag/source accessor rows while preserving prototype property insertion order. Added a builtins-lib bootstrap guard test covering RegExp.escape, RegExp[Symbol.species], RegExp.prototype.constructor, exec, Symbol.match, and global. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-builtins --lib; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests; cargo fmt --all --check; git diff --check; cargo clippy -p lyng-js-builtins --lib --all-features -- -W clippy::pedantic (exits 0 with the existing 555-warning backlog).

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Moved Date constructor and Date.prototype descriptor installation out of public.rs into public/families/date.rs. The Date family now owns its descriptor atoms and grouped static, formatter, getter, setter, conversion, and Symbol.toPrimitive rows while preserving Date.prototype property insertion order. Added a builtins-lib bootstrap guard test covering Date.now, Date.prototype.constructor, toString, getTime, setFullYear, and Symbol.toPrimitive. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-builtins --lib; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests; cargo fmt --all --check; git diff --check; cargo clippy -p lyng-js-builtins --lib --all-features -- -W clippy::pedantic (exits 0 with the existing 555-warning backlog).

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Moved Number, Math, BigInt, Boolean, and Symbol descriptor installation out of public.rs into public/families/primitives.rs. The primitive family now owns grouped Number static/prototype rows, Math method/constant/tag rows, BigInt/Boolean prototype rows, Symbol well-known-symbol constants, Symbol prototype rows, and local descriptor atoms. Added a builtins-lib bootstrap guard test covering Number.isFinite/NaN/prototype toString, Math.abs/toStringTag, BigInt.asIntN/prototype toString, Boolean.prototype.toString, Symbol.for/hasInstance/prototype toPrimitive/description. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-builtins --lib; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests; cargo fmt --all --check; git diff --check; cargo clippy -p lyng-js-builtins --lib --all-features -- -W clippy::pedantic (exits 0 with the existing 541-warning backlog).

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Moved Promise, DisposableStack, and AsyncDisposableStack descriptor installation out of public.rs into public/families/promises.rs. The promise/disposal family now owns Promise static/prototype rows, Symbol.species, DisposableStack prototype rows including disposed/Symbol.dispose, and AsyncDisposableStack prototype rows including disposeAsync/Symbol.asyncDispose. The empty constructor descriptor tables were dropped because they were no-op tables. Added a builtins-lib bootstrap guard test covering Promise.resolve, Promise[Symbol.species], Promise.prototype.then/toStringTag, DisposableStack.prototype constructor/use/disposed/Symbol.dispose, and AsyncDisposableStack.prototype constructor/disposeAsync/Symbol.asyncDispose. Verification: cargo test -p lyng-js-builtins --lib; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests; cargo fmt --all --check; git diff --check; cargo clippy -p lyng-js-builtins --lib --all-features -- -W clippy::pedantic (exits 0 with the existing 540-warning backlog).

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Moved binary-data descriptor installation out of public.rs into public/families/binary_data.rs. The binary-data family now owns ArrayBuffer, SharedArrayBuffer, Atomics, DataView, %TypedArray%, and concrete typed-array descriptor rows, with public.rs reduced to an orchestration call for that family. Added a builtins-lib bootstrap guard test covering ArrayBuffer.isView/species/prototype descriptors, Atomics.add/toStringTag, DataView.prototype accessors/methods, %TypedArray%.from/species/toStringTag, and Uint8Array BYTES_PER_ELEMENT/prototype values/iterator descriptors. Verification: cargo check -p lyng-js-builtins --lib; cargo test -p lyng-js-builtins --lib; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests; cargo fmt --all --check; git diff --check; cargo clippy -p lyng-js-builtins --lib --all-features -- -W clippy::pedantic (exits 0 with the existing 540-warning backlog).

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Collapsed the remaining public.rs bootstrap orchestration by moving family allocation into families::install_public_builtin_families and descriptor installation into families::install_public_family_descriptors. public.rs now coordinates scaffolding, prototype linking, intrinsic registration, Temporal object installation, cache insertion, and one family-descriptor call instead of manually owning the family allocation and descriptor sequences. This also removes the clippy too_many_lines warning for ensure_public_realm_builtins. Verification: cargo check -p lyng-js-builtins --lib; cargo test -p lyng-js-builtins --lib; cargo test -p lyng-js-tests; cargo fmt --all --check; git diff --check; cargo clippy -p lyng-js-builtins --lib --all-features -- -W clippy::pedantic (exits 0 with the existing 538-warning backlog).

title: Split public builtin bootstrap installers by family
Split public builtin dispatch by semantic family
description: Reduce crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch.rs by moving dispatch groups into family modules, following the existing temporal dispatch split, without changing builtin IDs or call ABI.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Extracted Object and Object.prototype public builtin dispatch routing into public/dispatch/objects.rs, leaving implementation bodies in the existing dispatch owner for now. The parent dispatch function now delegates Object routing through the family module, matching the existing Temporal split shape. comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com

This includes Number, Math, BigInt, Boolean, and Symbol builtin bodies, Math.sumPrecise iterator handling, BigInt width helpers, and Math.random time seeding. dispatch.rs keeps shared coercion/format helpers still used by JSON/string/language/array paths plus the primitive wrapper constructor used by String. comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Moved Error public builtin implementation bodies into crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/error_objects.rs. This includes Error and native error constructors, AggregateError, SuppressedError, Error.prototype.toString, cause installation, optional message coercion, and SuppressedError creation helpers. dispatch.rs now routes error IDs through error_objects before language support; language.rs no longer owns error routing. comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com

This includes DisposableStack, AsyncDisposableStack, disposal-scope helper builtins, async disposal resumption, and shared disposal error chaining through the error_objects SuppressedError helper. dispatch.rs now routes disposal IDs directly before language support, and language.rs is reduced to module-source/global builtin routing. Verification: cargo check -p lyng-js-builtins --lib; cargo test -p lyng-js-builtins --lib (32 passed); cargo test -p lyng-js-tests (877 passed); cargo fmt --all --check; git diff --check; cargo clippy -p lyng-js-builtins --lib --all-features -- -W clippy::pedantic (exits 0 with existing warning backlog, 529 builtins warnings).

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Moved Promise public builtin implementation bodies into crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/promises.rs. This includes Promise constructor/prototype/static builtins, capability executor and resolving functions, combinator element builtins, combinator algorithms, species/default constructor helpers, then invocation, reaction jobs, and Promise.any AggregateError construction. Generic iterator/array/data-property helpers stayed in dispatch.rs because arrays, JSON, object reflection, error objects, and primitive dispatch already depend on that shared surface. Atomics waitAsync now calls the Promise owner module explicitly, and disposal imports the Promise helper API from the Promise module instead of the parent monolith. Verification: cargo check -p lyng-js-builtins --lib; cargo test -p lyng-js-builtins --lib (32 passed); cargo test -p lyng-js-tests (877 passed); cargo fmt --all --check; git diff --check; cargo clippy -p lyng-js-builtins --lib --all-features -- -W clippy::pedantic (exits 0 with existing warning backlog, 529 builtins warnings).

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Moved iterator state support and iterator implementation bodies into crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/iterators.rs. This includes ArrayIterationKind, array/map/set/string iterator slot constants, iterator result allocation, iterator object allocation, iterator slot access/update helpers, %IteratorPrototype% @@iterator, ArrayIterator.prototype.next, StringIterator.prototype.next, and array/string/typed-array iterator factory builtins. Arrays, strings, collections, and the remaining typed-array code now import the iterator owner module explicitly instead of reaching through dispatch.rs. Verification: cargo check -p lyng-js-builtins --lib; cargo test -p lyng-js-builtins --lib (32 passed); cargo test -p lyng-js-tests (877 passed); cargo fmt --all --check; git diff --check; cargo clippy -p lyng-js-builtins --lib --all-features -- -W clippy::pedantic (exits 0 with existing warning backlog, 529 builtins warnings).

title: Split public builtin dispatch by semantic family
Split public builtin bootstrap installers by family
description: Move public realm builtin installation out of ensure_public_realm_builtins into family-owned modules under crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/families/. Keep public.rs as a thin orchestrator and preserve existing builtin IDs, handles, descriptors, and realm behavior.
acceptance: ensure_public_realm_builtins delegates to family installers; extracted families have focused guard tests where practical; cargo test -p lyng-js-builtins --lib and formatting pass.
close_reason: Completed in codex/lyng-2gmp-quality: public builtin bootstrap installers split by family.

title: Burn down builtins clippy warning backlog
Recover Lyng JS architecture and readability
description:
Create a dedicated quality-recovery track for the Lyng JS engine. Recent Test262 conformance work has improved coverage, but modularity and readability have regressed enough that future conformance fixes are becoming harder to review safely.

Review findings to preserve:

1. Builtin bootstrap/dispatch is too large to review safely. `crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public.rs:2226` contains `ensure_public_realm_builtins`, a 10k+ line bootstrap function. It sits beside large hand-maintained object lookup, metadata, and dispatch chains. Builtin fixes currently require synchronized edits across IDs, handles, install code, metadata, and dispatch.

2. Abstract operation ownership is split. `crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs:703` has a `Get` path that directly enters object storage while proxy-trap-aware paths live elsewhere. Correctness depends on callers choosing the right helper, which conflicts with the documented architecture goal that abstract operations have one owner and one semantic surface.

3. Dynamic compilation is VM-owned. `crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/builtin_dispatch.rs:758` and nearby VM code implement Function constructor compilation, indirect eval, direct eval, and script evaluation as separate VM flows. The docs call for one shared dynamic-compilation service/cache contract.

4. The documented clippy gate currently fails. `crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/temporal.rs:1965` contains a real Temporal comparison bug: `temporal_exact_time_unit_order` returns `u8`, so comparing it with `<= 0` is only true for zero. The JS engine also has hundreds of pedantic warnings, mostly concentrated in builtins.

The goal is not a broad rewrite. The goal is to establish guardrails, extract canonical ownership points, and keep Test262 progress while reducing review risk.
acceptance: A fresh agent can start quality-recovery work from this issue without needing the original review thread. Acceptance requires: (1) the current hard clippy failure is fixed or explicitly tracked as a child issue; (2) a canonical builtin registry/bootstrap/dispatch plan exists or is implemented incrementally without changing builtin IDs; (3) proxy-aware object operation ownership is consolidated behind one `lyng-js-ops` surface; (4) Function constructor, direct eval, indirect eval, and script evaluation share a documented dynamic-compilation service/cache contract; (5) owner-layer tests cover extracted behavior before or alongside refactors; (6) focused Test262 slices are run for any semantics touched.
notes: Suggested child issues: builtin registry/bootstrap extraction; proxy-aware object operation surface; dynamic-compilation service extraction; clippy/readability baseline for builtins; owner-layer tests for proxy/eval/bootstrap invariants. Do not close this epic without explicit user approval.
close_reason: Completed cleanup run in codex/lyng-2gmp-quality: plan checked in, builtin metadata/bootstrap/dispatch split, ops/proxy surface consolidated, dynamic compilation extracted, and clippy backlog reduced with verification including full Test262.

Verification run: cargo fmt --all --check (passed); cargo clippy -p lyng-js-builtins --lib --all-features -- -W clippy::pedantic (passed, existing warning backlog remains); cargo test -p lyng-js-builtins --lib (6 passed); cargo test -p lyng-js-tests temporal_instant (23 passed).

description: Remove the legacy js3_/JS3_ naming surface from Lyng JS builtin identifier helpers and namespace constants. Address Lyng JS code quality review findings
description: Parent epic for the comprehensive Lyng JS code review findings from April 25 2026. The review identified guest-triggered panic risk, panic-based compiler and bytecode limits, unclear proxy-aware object operation boundaries, collectible bootstrap atoms, VM ownership of spec-like builtin semantics, and oversized core modules. This epic groups the remediation work without marking any child as started.
acceptance: All child review findings are either fixed with focused verification, explicitly deferred with rationale, or superseded by more specific issues. Documentation and tests reflect the final ownership and safety contracts.

title: Address Lyng JS code quality review findings
Split oversized Lyng JS core modules along ownership boundaries
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. Several core Lyng JS files are thousands of lines and mix multiple major responsibilities, contrary to the repo-level Rust module guidance. This makes ownership, review, and targeted verification harder.
acceptance: Create an incremental module split plan that preserves public APIs and crate boundaries. Split at least the highest-impact files by domain or object family without broad semantic rewrites. Keep lib.rs and main.rs thin, expose intentional public APIs via pub use, and run targeted tests for every moved subsystem.
notes: Observed file sizes from review: crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/temporal.rs 9979 lines, crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs 7210 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/temporal.rs 6169 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs 4327 lines, crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/builtin_dispatch.rs 3869 lines, crates/lyng-js/gc/src/arena.rs 3142 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/bootstrap.rs 3132 lines, crates/lyng-js/objects/src/internal_methods.rs 2395 lines, crates/lyng-js/env/src/agent.rs 2234 lines, crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs 2215 lines.

title: Separate proxy-aware object operations from ordinary-only helpers
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs exposes proxy-aware *_in_context operations next to exported helpers with ECMAScript names such as has_property and get that go directly to ObjectRuntime internal methods. ObjectRuntime proxy branches forward to the proxy target without traps, so these helpers are ordinary-only in practice but easy to call as if they were spec operations.
acceptance: Ordinary-only helpers are renamed, scoped, or documented so they cannot be mistaken for proxy-observable ECMAScript operations. Guest-observable VM and builtin paths use proxy-aware context operations unless an ordinary-only path is explicitly required by the spec algorithm. Focused tests cover representative proxy traps for get, has, set, deleteProperty, defineProperty, getOwnPropertyDescriptor, ownKeys, getPrototypeOf, and setPrototypeOf through affected VM and builtin paths.
notes: Primary evidence: crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs:838-879 direct public helpers. Proxy-aware counterparts live around object.rs:99-234. ObjectRuntime Proxy forwarding appears in crates/lyng-js/objects/src/internal_methods.rs around get_prototype_of line 17, get_own_property line 132, define_own_property line 168, has_property line 213, get line 241, set line 272, delete line 306, own_property_keys line 340. Review probe showed Array.prototype.indexOf.call(proxy, ...) currently passes through the public builtin path, so this is a boundary and auditability issue rather than that specific failing behavior.

title: Make bytecode builder source limits fallible
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. crates/lyng-js/bytecode/src/builder.rs still models register, operand, instruction stream, jump, constant, child-function, capture, exception-handler, feedback, and safepoint limits with expect or panic. These limits are ultimately controlled by source size or program shape and should produce structured lowering or compile errors rather than aborting the process.
acceptance: Public bytecode builder APIs used by compiler lowering are fallible for source-size and program-shape limits. Compiler plumbs these failures into structured lowering diagnostics. Regression tests cover at least register overflow, narrow call operand overflow, jump distance or instruction stream overflow where practical, and oversized constant or child-function tables where practical. No guest-triggerable panic remains for these limits.
notes: Primary evidence: crates/lyng-js/bytecode/src/builder.rs:218-222 alloc_register panics. Related evidence: emit_abc around line 317, emit_call around line 403, emit_tail_call around line 425, emit_construct around line 443, patch_jump_to around line 457, add_constant/add_child_function/add_capture/add_exception_handler around lines 501-535. Compiler has LoweringError::RegisterOverflow but builder APIs are not uniformly fallible.

title: Move spec-like builtin semantics out of VM builtin dispatch
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. VM builtin dispatch still implements Object, Array, and String spec-like algorithms and internal shim behavior directly. The Lyng JS architecture says builtins and ops own semantic algorithms while the VM executes bytecode and provides narrow execution services. Keeping spec-visible behavior in the VM increases drift risk against public builtin implementations.
acceptance: Spec-like Object, Array, and String builtin algorithms live in lyng-js-builtins or lyng-js-ops rather than VM builtin dispatch. VM-owned dispatch remains limited to execution-sensitive services such as direct eval, call bridging, and bytecode-specific machinery. Tests demonstrate that internal shims and public builtins share the same semantic implementation or explicit delegated helper.
notes: Primary evidence: crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/builtin_dispatch.rs:1102-1118 implements object_has_own_property_builtin. Additional examples include array_index_of_builtin around line 1152, array_push_builtin around line 1290, array_pop_builtin around line 1330, and object_to_string_builtin around line 1372. Internal shims are allocated in crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/internal.rs:337-347 and dispatched through InternalBuiltinDispatchContext around internal.rs:954.

title: Intern bootstrap builtin descriptor atoms as permanent metadata
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. Bootstrap and public builtin descriptor names are interned with intern_collectible even though AtomTable documents permanent frontend and metadata atoms versus collectible runtime atomization. Immutable realm metadata should not depend on collectible atom liveness tracing and should not add avoidable GC sweep work.
acceptance: Bootstrap descriptor names, builtin metadata names, and realm-owned spec names use permanent atoms or are promoted to permanent during bootstrap. Runtime guest-created strings and property keys continue to use collectible atomization where appropriate. Add a focused test or invariant that realm/bootstrap descriptor atoms remain permanent after bootstrap and after an atom sweep.
notes: Primary evidence: crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/families/arrays.rs:392-437 uses atoms.intern_collectible for Array descriptor names. AtomTable lifetime contract is documented at crates/lyng-js/common/src/atom.rs:7-9, with permanent intern at atom.rs:431 and collectible intern at atom.rs:435. Similar collectible bootstrap names should be audited across builtins family installers and internal builtin metadata.

title: Make bytecode builder source limits fallible
Separate proxy-aware object operations from ordinary-only helpers
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs exposes proxy-aware *_in_context operations next to exported helpers with ECMAScript names such as has_property and get that go directly to ObjectRuntime internal methods. ObjectRuntime proxy branches forward to the proxy target without traps, so these helpers are ordinary-only in practice but easy to call as if they were spec operations.
acceptance: Ordinary-only helpers are renamed, scoped, or documented so they cannot be mistaken for proxy-observable ECMAScript operations. Guest-observable VM and builtin paths use proxy-aware context operations unless an ordinary-only path is explicitly required by the spec algorithm. Focused tests cover representative proxy traps for get, has, set, deleteProperty, defineProperty, getOwnPropertyDescriptor, ownKeys, getPrototypeOf, and setPrototypeOf through affected VM and builtin paths.
notes: Primary evidence: crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs:838-879 direct public helpers. Proxy-aware counterparts live around object.rs:99-234. ObjectRuntime Proxy forwarding appears in crates/lyng-js/objects/src/internal_methods.rs around get_prototype_of line 17, get_own_property line 132, define_own_property line 168, has_property line 213, get line 241, set line 272, delete line 306, own_property_keys line 340. Review probe showed Array.prototype.indexOf.call(proxy, ...) currently passes through the public builtin path, so this is a boundary and auditability issue rather than that specific failing behavior.

title: Separate proxy-aware object operations from ordinary-only helpers
Move spec-like builtin semantics out of VM builtin dispatch
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. VM builtin dispatch still implements Object, Array, and String spec-like algorithms and internal shim behavior directly. The Lyng JS architecture says builtins and ops own semantic algorithms while the VM executes bytecode and provides narrow execution services. Keeping spec-visible behavior in the VM increases drift risk against public builtin implementations.
acceptance: Spec-like Object, Array, and String builtin algorithms live in lyng-js-builtins or lyng-js-ops rather than VM builtin dispatch. VM-owned dispatch remains limited to execution-sensitive services such as direct eval, call bridging, and bytecode-specific machinery. Tests demonstrate that internal shims and public builtins share the same semantic implementation or explicit delegated helper.
notes: Primary evidence: crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/builtin_dispatch.rs:1102-1118 implements object_has_own_property_builtin. Additional examples include array_index_of_builtin around line 1152, array_push_builtin around line 1290, array_pop_builtin around line 1330, and object_to_string_builtin around line 1372. Internal shims are allocated in crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/internal.rs:337-347 and dispatched through InternalBuiltinDispatchContext around internal.rs:954.

title: Move spec-like builtin semantics out of VM builtin dispatch
Align lyng-js-test262 runner with Test262 interpreting procedure
description: Audit against tc39/test262 INTERPRETING.md found that tools/lyng-js-test262 does not fully follow the expected execution procedure. Known gaps: default tests are not run in both non-strict and strict variants; harness base files are loaded as sta.js then assert.js instead of assert.js then sta.js; raw tests are skipped instead of executed once without harness or source modification; async tests use a local  shim rather than doneprintHandle.js plus print-observed completion/failure messages;  is partial and lacks explicit policy for AbstractModuleSource, agent, and IsHTMLDDA-tagged tests. Relevant files include tools/lyng-js-test262/src/helpers.rs, metadata.rs, selection.rs, execution.rs, extensions.rs, and tests under tools/lyng-js-test262/tests/.
acceptance: Focused tests cover strict variant expansion, raw handling, harness order, async print completion/failure, and unsupported host feature policy.

Split oversized Lyng JS core modules along ownership boundaries
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. Several core Lyng JS files are thousands of lines and mix multiple major responsibilities, contrary to the repo-level Rust module guidance. This makes ownership, review, and targeted verification harder.
acceptance: Create an incremental module split plan that preserves public APIs and crate boundaries. Split at least the highest-impact files by domain or object family without broad semantic rewrites. Keep lib.rs and main.rs thin, expose intentional public APIs via pub use, and run targeted tests for every moved subsystem.
notes: Observed file sizes from review: crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/temporal.rs 9979 lines, crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs 7210 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/temporal.rs 6169 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs 4327 lines, crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/builtin_dispatch.rs 3869 lines, crates/lyng-js/gc/src/arena.rs 3142 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/bootstrap.rs 3132 lines, crates/lyng-js/objects/src/internal_methods.rs 2395 lines, crates/lyng-js/env/src/agent.rs 2234 lines, crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs 2215 lines.

title: Split oversized Lyng JS core modules along ownership boundaries
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. Several core Lyng JS files are thousands of lines and mix multiple major responsibilities, contrary to the repo-level Rust module guidance. This makes ownership, review, and targeted verification harder.
acceptance: Create an incremental module split plan that preserves public APIs and crate boundaries. Split at least the highest-impact files by domain or object family without broad semantic rewrites. Keep lib.rs and main.rs thin, expose intentional public APIs via pub use, and run targeted tests for every moved subsystem.
notes: Observed file sizes from review: crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/temporal.rs 9979 lines, crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs 7210 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/temporal.rs 6169 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs 4327 lines, crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/builtin_dispatch.rs 3869 lines, crates/lyng-js/gc/src/arena.rs 3142 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/bootstrap.rs 3132 lines, crates/lyng-js/objects/src/internal_methods.rs 2395 lines, crates/lyng-js/env/src/agent.rs 2234 lines, crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs 2215 lines.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Implemented an incremental module split plan at docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-26-lyng-js-module-split.md. Split crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs into ids, marker, and builtin_ids modules, with builtin ID helpers separated under builtin_ids/*.rs. Split crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs table ownership into public/metadata/core.rs, binary_data.rs, and temporal.rs while preserving the lookup facade. Verification: cargo fmt --all; cargo test -p lyng-js-types (26 passed, doc-tests 0); cargo test -p lyng-js-builtins --lib (32 passed); cargo fmt --all --check.

title: Split oversized Lyng JS core modules along ownership boundaries
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. Several core Lyng JS files are thousands of lines and mix multiple major responsibilities, contrary to the repo-level Rust module guidance. This makes ownership, review, and targeted verification harder.
acceptance: Create an incremental module split plan that preserves public APIs and crate boundaries. Split at least the highest-impact files by domain or object family without broad semantic rewrites. Keep lib.rs and main.rs thin, expose intentional public APIs via pub use, and run targeted tests for every moved subsystem.
notes: Observed file sizes from review: crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/temporal.rs 9979 lines, crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs 7210 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/temporal.rs 6169 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs 4327 lines, crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/builtin_dispatch.rs 3869 lines, crates/lyng-js/gc/src/arena.rs 3142 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/bootstrap.rs 3132 lines, crates/lyng-js/objects/src/internal_methods.rs 2395 lines, crates/lyng-js/env/src/agent.rs 2234 lines, crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs 2215 lines.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Implemented an incremental module split plan at docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-26-lyng-js-module-split.md. Split crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs into ids, marker, and builtin_ids modules, with builtin ID helpers separated under builtin_ids/*.rs. Split crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs table ownership into public/metadata/core.rs, binary_data.rs, and temporal.rs while preserving the lookup facade. Verification: cargo fmt --all; cargo test -p lyng-js-types (26 passed, doc-tests 0); cargo test -p lyng-js-builtins --lib (32 passed); cargo fmt --all --check.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Continued module-split checkpoint: split lyng-js-types builtin id helpers into second-level owner modules, moved Temporal public metadata out of the bootstrap file and split it by family, split core public metadata by family, and extracted Temporal.Instant, Temporal.PlainTime, and Temporal.PlainMonthDay public bootstrap wiring into child modules. Verification passed: cargo fmt --all --check; cargo test -p lyng-js-types (26 passed, doc-tests 0); cargo test -p lyng-js-builtins --lib (32 passed); git diff --check.

title: Split oversized Lyng JS core modules along ownership boundaries
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. Several core Lyng JS files are thousands of lines and mix multiple major responsibilities, contrary to the repo-level Rust module guidance. This makes ownership, review, and targeted verification harder.
acceptance: Create an incremental module split plan that preserves public APIs and crate boundaries. Split at least the highest-impact files by domain or object family without broad semantic rewrites. Keep lib.rs and main.rs thin, expose intentional public APIs via pub use, and run targeted tests for every moved subsystem.
notes: Observed file sizes from review: crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/temporal.rs 9979 lines, crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs 7210 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/temporal.rs 6169 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs 4327 lines, crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/builtin_dispatch.rs 3869 lines, crates/lyng-js/gc/src/arena.rs 3142 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/bootstrap.rs 3132 lines, crates/lyng-js/objects/src/internal_methods.rs 2395 lines, crates/lyng-js/env/src/agent.rs 2234 lines, crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs 2215 lines.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Implemented an incremental module split plan at docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-26-lyng-js-module-split.md. Split crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs into ids, marker, and builtin_ids modules, with builtin ID helpers separated under builtin_ids/*.rs. Split crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs table ownership into public/metadata/core.rs, binary_data.rs, and temporal.rs while preserving the lookup facade. Verification: cargo fmt --all; cargo test -p lyng-js-types (26 passed, doc-tests 0); cargo test -p lyng-js-builtins --lib (32 passed); cargo fmt --all --check.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com

Verification passed: cargo fmt --all --check; cargo test -p lyng-js-types (26 passed, doc-tests 0); cargo test -p lyng-js-builtins --lib (32 passed); git diff --check.

title: Split oversized Lyng JS core modules along ownership boundaries
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. Several core Lyng JS files are thousands of lines and mix multiple major responsibilities, contrary to the repo-level Rust module guidance. This makes ownership, review, and targeted verification harder.
acceptance: Create an incremental module split plan that preserves public APIs and crate boundaries. Split at least the highest-impact files by domain or object family without broad semantic rewrites. Keep lib.rs and main.rs thin, expose intentional public APIs via pub use, and run targeted tests for every moved subsystem.
notes: Observed file sizes from review: crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/temporal.rs 9979 lines, crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs 7210 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/temporal.rs 6169 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs 4327 lines, crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/builtin_dispatch.rs 3869 lines, crates/lyng-js/gc/src/arena.rs 3142 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/bootstrap.rs 3132 lines, crates/lyng-js/objects/src/internal_methods.rs 2395 lines, crates/lyng-js/env/src/agent.rs 2234 lines, crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs 2215 lines.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Implemented an incremental module split plan at docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-26-lyng-js-module-split.md. Split crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs into ids, marker, and builtin_ids modules, with builtin ID helpers separated under builtin_ids/*.rs. Split crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs table ownership into public/metadata/core.rs, binary_data.rs, and temporal.rs while preserving the lookup facade. Verification: cargo fmt --all; cargo test -p lyng-js-types (26 passed, doc-tests 0); cargo test -p lyng-js-builtins --lib (32 passed); cargo fmt --all --check.

title: Split oversized Lyng JS core modules along ownership boundaries
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. Several core Lyng JS files are thousands of lines and mix multiple major responsibilities, contrary to the repo-level Rust module guidance. This makes ownership, review, and targeted verification harder.
acceptance: Create an incremental module split plan that preserves public APIs and crate boundaries. Split at least the highest-impact files by domain or object family without broad semantic rewrites. Keep lib.rs and main.rs thin, expose intentional public APIs via pub use, and run targeted tests for every moved subsystem.
notes: Observed file sizes from review: crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/temporal.rs 9979 lines, crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs 7210 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/temporal.rs 6169 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs 4327 lines, crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/builtin_dispatch.rs 3869 lines, crates/lyng-js/gc/src/arena.rs 3142 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/bootstrap.rs 3132 lines, crates/lyng-js/objects/src/internal_methods.rs 2395 lines, crates/lyng-js/env/src/agent.rs 2234 lines, crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs 2215 lines.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Implemented an incremental module split plan at docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-26-lyng-js-module-split.md. Split crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs into ids, marker, and builtin_ids modules, with builtin ID helpers separated under builtin_ids/*.rs. Split crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs table ownership into public/metadata/core.rs, binary_data.rs, and temporal.rs while preserving the lookup facade. title: Split oversized Lyng JS core modules along ownership boundaries
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. Several core Lyng JS files are thousands of lines and mix multiple major responsibilities, contrary to the repo-level Rust module guidance. This makes ownership, review, and targeted verification harder.
acceptance: Create an incremental module split plan that preserves public APIs and crate boundaries. Split at least the highest-impact files by domain or object family without broad semantic rewrites. Keep lib.rs and main.rs thin, expose intentional public APIs via pub use, and run targeted tests for every moved subsystem.
notes: Observed file sizes from review: crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/temporal.rs 9979 lines, crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs 7210 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/temporal.rs 6169 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs 4327 lines, crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/builtin_dispatch.rs 3869 lines, crates/lyng-js/gc/src/arena.rs 3142 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/bootstrap.rs 3132 lines, crates/lyng-js/objects/src/internal_methods.rs 2395 lines, crates/lyng-js/env/src/agent.rs 2234 lines, crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs 2215 lines.

title: Split oversized Lyng JS core modules along ownership boundaries
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. Several core Lyng JS files are thousands of lines and mix multiple major responsibilities, contrary to the repo-level Rust module guidance. This makes ownership, review, and targeted verification harder.
acceptance: Create an incremental module split plan that preserves public APIs and crate boundaries. Split at least the highest-impact files by domain or object family without broad semantic rewrites. Keep lib.rs and main.rs thin, expose intentional public APIs via pub use, and run targeted tests for every moved subsystem.
notes: Observed file sizes from review: crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/temporal.rs 9979 lines, crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs 7210 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/temporal.rs 6169 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs 4327 lines, crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/builtin_dispatch.rs 3869 lines, crates/lyng-js/gc/src/arena.rs 3142 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/bootstrap.rs 3132 lines, crates/lyng-js/objects/src/internal_methods.rs 2395 lines, crates/lyng-js/env/src/agent.rs 2234 lines, crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs 2215 lines.

title: Split oversized Lyng JS core modules along ownership boundaries
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. Several core Lyng JS files are thousands of lines and mix multiple major responsibilities, contrary to the repo-level Rust module guidance. This makes ownership, review, and targeted verification harder.
acceptance: Create an incremental module split plan that preserves public APIs and crate boundaries. Split at least the highest-impact files by domain or object family without broad semantic rewrites. Keep lib.rs and main.rs thin, expose intentional public APIs via pub use, and run targeted tests for every moved subsystem.
notes: Observed file sizes from review: crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/temporal.rs 9979 lines, crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs 7210 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/temporal.rs 6169 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs 4327 lines, crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/builtin_dispatch.rs 3869 lines, crates/lyng-js/gc/src/arena.rs 3142 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/bootstrap.rs 3132 lines, crates/lyng-js/objects/src/internal_methods.rs 2395 lines, crates/lyng-js/env/src/agent.rs 2234 lines, crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs 2215 lines.

comments.text: Implemented an incremental module split plan at docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-26-lyng-js-module-split.md. Split crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs table ownership into public/metadata/core.rs, binary_data.rs, and temporal.rs while preserving the lookup facade. title: Split oversized Lyng JS core modules along ownership boundaries
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. Several core Lyng JS files are thousands of lines and mix multiple major responsibilities, contrary to the repo-level Rust module guidance. This makes ownership, review, and targeted verification harder.
acceptance: Create an incremental module split plan that preserves public APIs and crate boundaries. Split at least the highest-impact files by domain or object family without broad semantic rewrites. Keep lib.rs and main.rs thin, expose intentional public APIs via pub use, and run targeted tests for every moved subsystem.
notes: Observed file sizes from review: crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/temporal.rs 9979 lines, crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs 7210 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/temporal.rs 6169 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs 4327 lines, crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/builtin_dispatch.rs 3869 lines, crates/lyng-js/gc/src/arena.rs 3142 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/bootstrap.rs 3132 lines, crates/lyng-js/objects/src/internal_methods.rs 2395 lines, crates/lyng-js/env/src/agent.rs 2234 lines, crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs 2215 lines.

title: Split oversized Lyng JS core modules along ownership boundaries
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. Several core Lyng JS files are thousands of lines and mix multiple major responsibilities, contrary to the repo-level Rust module guidance. This makes ownership, review, and targeted verification harder.
acceptance: Create an incremental module split plan that preserves public APIs and crate boundaries. Split at least the highest-impact files by domain or object family without broad semantic rewrites. Keep lib.rs and main.rs thin, expose intentional public APIs via pub use, and run targeted tests for every moved subsystem.
notes: Observed file sizes from review: crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/temporal.rs 9979 lines, crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs 7210 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/temporal.rs 6169 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs 4327 lines, crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/builtin_dispatch.rs 3869 lines, crates/lyng-js/gc/src/arena.rs 3142 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/bootstrap.rs 3132 lines, crates/lyng-js/objects/src/internal_methods.rs 2395 lines, crates/lyng-js/env/src/agent.rs 2234 lines, crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs 2215 lines.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Implemented an incremental module split plan at docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-26-lyng-js-module-split.md. Split crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs into ids, marker, and builtin_ids modules, with builtin ID helpers separated under builtin_ids/*.rs. Split crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs table ownership into public/metadata/core.rs, binary_data.rs, and temporal.rs while preserving the lookup facade. title: Split oversized Lyng JS core modules along ownership boundaries
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. Several core Lyng JS files are thousands of lines and mix multiple major responsibilities, contrary to the repo-level Rust module guidance. This makes ownership, review, and targeted verification harder.
acceptance: Create an incremental module split plan that preserves public APIs and crate boundaries. Split at least the highest-impact files by domain or object family without broad semantic rewrites. Keep lib.rs and main.rs thin, expose intentional public APIs via pub use, and run targeted tests for every moved subsystem.
notes: Observed file sizes from review: crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/temporal.rs 9979 lines, crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs 7210 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/temporal.rs 6169 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs 4327 lines, crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/builtin_dispatch.rs 3869 lines, crates/lyng-js/gc/src/arena.rs 3142 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/bootstrap.rs 3132 lines, crates/lyng-js/objects/src/internal_methods.rs 2395 lines, crates/lyng-js/env/src/agent.rs 2234 lines, crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs 2215 lines.

Split crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs table ownership into public/metadata/core.rs, binary_data.rs, and temporal.rs while preserving the lookup facade. title: Split oversized Lyng JS core modules along ownership boundaries
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. Several core Lyng JS files are thousands of lines and mix multiple major responsibilities, contrary to the repo-level Rust module guidance. This makes ownership, review, and targeted verification harder.
acceptance: Create an incremental module split plan that preserves public APIs and crate boundaries. Split at least the highest-impact files by domain or object family without broad semantic rewrites. Keep lib.rs and main.rs thin, expose intentional public APIs via pub use, and run targeted tests for every moved subsystem.
notes: Observed file sizes from review: crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/temporal.rs 9979 lines, crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs 7210 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/temporal.rs 6169 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs 4327 lines, crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/builtin_dispatch.rs 3869 lines, crates/lyng-js/gc/src/arena.rs 3142 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/bootstrap.rs 3132 lines, crates/lyng-js/objects/src/internal_methods.rs 2395 lines, crates/lyng-js/env/src/agent.rs 2234 lines, crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs 2215 lines.

comments.text: Implemented an incremental module split plan at docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-26-lyng-js-module-split.md. Split crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs into ids, marker, and builtin_ids modules, with builtin ID helpers separated under builtin_ids/*.rs. Split crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs table ownership into public/metadata/core.rs, binary_data.rs, and temporal.rs while preserving the lookup facade. title: Split oversized Lyng JS core modules along ownership boundaries
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. Several core Lyng JS files are thousands of lines and mix multiple major responsibilities, contrary to the repo-level Rust module guidance. This makes ownership, review, and targeted verification harder.
acceptance: Create an incremental module split plan that preserves public APIs and crate boundaries. Split at least the highest-impact files by domain or object family without broad semantic rewrites. Keep lib.rs and main.rs thin, expose intentional public APIs via pub use, and run targeted tests for every moved subsystem.
notes: Observed file sizes from review: crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/temporal.rs 9979 lines, crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs 7210 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/temporal.rs 6169 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs 4327 lines, crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/builtin_dispatch.rs 3869 lines, crates/lyng-js/gc/src/arena.rs 3142 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/bootstrap.rs 3132 lines, crates/lyng-js/objects/src/internal_methods.rs 2395 lines, crates/lyng-js/env/src/agent.rs 2234 lines, crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs 2215 lines.

Split crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs table ownership into public/metadata/core.rs, binary_data.rs, and temporal.rs while preserving the lookup facade. title: Split oversized Lyng JS core modules along ownership boundaries
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. Several core Lyng JS files are thousands of lines and mix multiple major responsibilities, contrary to the repo-level Rust module guidance. This makes ownership, review, and targeted verification harder.
acceptance: Create an incremental module split plan that preserves public APIs and crate boundaries. Split at least the highest-impact files by domain or object family without broad semantic rewrites. Keep lib.rs and main.rs thin, expose intentional public APIs via pub use, and run targeted tests for every moved subsystem.
notes: Observed file sizes from review: crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/temporal.rs 9979 lines, crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs 7210 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/temporal.rs 6169 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs 4327 lines, crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/builtin_dispatch.rs 3869 lines, crates/lyng-js/gc/src/arena.rs 3142 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/bootstrap.rs 3132 lines, crates/lyng-js/objects/src/internal_methods.rs 2395 lines, crates/lyng-js/env/src/agent.rs 2234 lines, crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs 2215 lines.

Split crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs table ownership into public/metadata/core.rs, binary_data.rs, and temporal.rs while preserving the lookup facade. title: Split oversized Lyng JS core modules along ownership boundaries
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. Several core Lyng JS files are thousands of lines and mix multiple major responsibilities, contrary to the repo-level Rust module guidance. This makes ownership, review, and targeted verification harder.
acceptance: Create an incremental module split plan that preserves public APIs and crate boundaries. Split at least the highest-impact files by domain or object family without broad semantic rewrites. Keep lib.rs and main.rs thin, expose intentional public APIs via pub use, and run targeted tests for every moved subsystem.
notes: Observed file sizes from review: crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/temporal.rs 9979 lines, crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs 7210 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/temporal.rs 6169 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs 4327 lines, crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/builtin_dispatch.rs 3869 lines, crates/lyng-js/gc/src/arena.rs 3142 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/bootstrap.rs 3132 lines, crates/lyng-js/objects/src/internal_methods.rs 2395 lines, crates/lyng-js/env/src/agent.rs 2234 lines, crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs 2215 lines.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Implemented an incremental module split plan at docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-26-lyng-js-module-split.md. Split crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs into ids, marker, and builtin_ids modules, with builtin ID helpers separated under builtin_ids/*.rs. Split crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs table ownership into public/metadata/core.rs, binary_data.rs, and temporal.rs while preserving the lookup facade. title: Split oversized Lyng JS core modules along ownership boundaries
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. Several core Lyng JS files are thousands of lines and mix multiple major responsibilities, contrary to the repo-level Rust module guidance. This makes ownership, review, and targeted verification harder.
acceptance: Create an incremental module split plan that preserves public APIs and crate boundaries. Split at least the highest-impact files by domain or object family without broad semantic rewrites. Keep lib.rs and main.rs thin, expose intentional public APIs via pub use, and run targeted tests for every moved subsystem.
notes: Observed file sizes from review: crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/temporal.rs 9979 lines, crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs 7210 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/temporal.rs 6169 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs 4327 lines, crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/builtin_dispatch.rs 3869 lines, crates/lyng-js/gc/src/arena.rs 3142 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/bootstrap.rs 3132 lines, crates/lyng-js/objects/src/internal_methods.rs 2395 lines, crates/lyng-js/env/src/agent.rs 2234 lines, crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs 2215 lines.

title: Split oversized Lyng JS core modules along ownership boundaries
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. Several core Lyng JS files are thousands of lines and mix multiple major responsibilities, contrary to the repo-level Rust module guidance. This makes ownership, review, and targeted verification harder.
acceptance: Create an incremental module split plan that preserves public APIs and crate boundaries. Split at least the highest-impact files by domain or object family without broad semantic rewrites. Keep lib.rs and main.rs thin, expose intentional public APIs via pub use, and run targeted tests for every moved subsystem.
notes: Observed file sizes from review: crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/temporal.rs 9979 lines, crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs 7210 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/temporal.rs 6169 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs 4327 lines, crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/builtin_dispatch.rs 3869 lines, crates/lyng-js/gc/src/arena.rs 3142 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/bootstrap.rs 3132 lines, crates/lyng-js/objects/src/internal_methods.rs 2395 lines, crates/lyng-js/env/src/agent.rs 2234 lines, crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs 2215 lines.

title: Split oversized Lyng JS core modules along ownership boundaries
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. Several core Lyng JS files are thousands of lines and mix multiple major responsibilities, contrary to the repo-level Rust module guidance. This makes ownership, review, and targeted verification harder.
acceptance: Create an incremental module split plan that preserves public APIs and crate boundaries. Split at least the highest-impact files by domain or object family without broad semantic rewrites. Keep lib.rs and main.rs thin, expose intentional public APIs via pub use, and run targeted tests for every moved subsystem.
notes: Observed file sizes from review: crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/temporal.rs 9979 lines, crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs 7210 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/temporal.rs 6169 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs 4327 lines, crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/builtin_dispatch.rs 3869 lines, crates/lyng-js/gc/src/arena.rs 3142 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/bootstrap.rs 3132 lines, crates/lyng-js/objects/src/internal_methods.rs 2395 lines, crates/lyng-js/env/src/agent.rs 2234 lines, crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs 2215 lines.

Split crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs table ownership into public/metadata/core.rs, binary_data.rs, and temporal.rs while preserving the lookup facade. title: Split oversized Lyng JS core modules along ownership boundaries
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. Several core Lyng JS files are thousands of lines and mix multiple major responsibilities, contrary to the repo-level Rust module guidance. This makes ownership, review, and targeted verification harder.
acceptance: Create an incremental module split plan that preserves public APIs and crate boundaries. Split at least the highest-impact files by domain or object family without broad semantic rewrites. Keep lib.rs and main.rs thin, expose intentional public APIs via pub use, and run targeted tests for every moved subsystem.
notes: Observed file sizes from review: crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/temporal.rs 9979 lines, crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs 7210 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/temporal.rs 6169 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs 4327 lines, crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/builtin_dispatch.rs 3869 lines, crates/lyng-js/gc/src/arena.rs 3142 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/bootstrap.rs 3132 lines, crates/lyng-js/objects/src/internal_methods.rs 2395 lines, crates/lyng-js/env/src/agent.rs 2234 lines, crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs 2215 lines.

title: Split oversized Lyng JS core modules along ownership boundaries
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. Several core Lyng JS files are thousands of lines and mix multiple major responsibilities, contrary to the repo-level Rust module guidance. This makes ownership, review, and targeted verification harder.
acceptance: Create an incremental module split plan that preserves public APIs and crate boundaries. Split at least the highest-impact files by domain or object family without broad semantic rewrites. Keep lib.rs and main.rs thin, expose intentional public APIs via pub use, and run targeted tests for every moved subsystem.
notes: Observed file sizes from review: crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/temporal.rs 9979 lines, crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs 7210 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/temporal.rs 6169 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs 4327 lines, crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/builtin_dispatch.rs 3869 lines, crates/lyng-js/gc/src/arena.rs 3142 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/bootstrap.rs 3132 lines, crates/lyng-js/objects/src/internal_methods.rs 2395 lines, crates/lyng-js/env/src/agent.rs 2234 lines, crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs 2215 lines.

title: Split oversized Lyng JS core modules along ownership boundaries
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. Several core Lyng JS files are thousands of lines and mix multiple major responsibilities, contrary to the repo-level Rust module guidance. This makes ownership, review, and targeted verification harder.
acceptance: Create an incremental module split plan that preserves public APIs and crate boundaries. Split at least the highest-impact files by domain or object family without broad semantic rewrites. Keep lib.rs and main.rs thin, expose intentional public APIs via pub use, and run targeted tests for every moved subsystem.
notes: Observed file sizes from review: crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/temporal.rs 9979 lines, crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs 7210 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/temporal.rs 6169 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs 4327 lines, crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/builtin_dispatch.rs 3869 lines, crates/lyng-js/gc/src/arena.rs 3142 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/bootstrap.rs 3132 lines, crates/lyng-js/objects/src/internal_methods.rs 2395 lines, crates/lyng-js/env/src/agent.rs 2234 lines, crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs 2215 lines.

title: Split oversized Lyng JS core modules along ownership boundaries
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. Several core Lyng JS files are thousands of lines and mix multiple major responsibilities, contrary to the repo-level Rust module guidance. This makes ownership, review, and targeted verification harder.
acceptance: Create an incremental module split plan that preserves public APIs and crate boundaries. Split at least the highest-impact files by domain or object family without broad semantic rewrites. Keep lib.rs and main.rs thin, expose intentional public APIs via pub use, and run targeted tests for every moved subsystem.
notes: Observed file sizes from review: crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/temporal.rs 9979 lines, crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs 7210 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/temporal.rs 6169 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs 4327 lines, crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/builtin_dispatch.rs 3869 lines, crates/lyng-js/gc/src/arena.rs 3142 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/bootstrap.rs 3132 lines, crates/lyng-js/objects/src/internal_methods.rs 2395 lines, crates/lyng-js/env/src/agent.rs 2234 lines, crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs 2215 lines.

title: Split oversized Lyng JS core modules along ownership boundaries
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. Several core Lyng JS files are thousands of lines and mix multiple major responsibilities, contrary to the repo-level Rust module guidance. This makes ownership, review, and targeted verification harder.
acceptance: Create an incremental module split plan that preserves public APIs and crate boundaries. Split at least the highest-impact files by domain or object family without broad semantic rewrites. Keep lib.rs and main.rs thin, expose intentional public APIs via pub use, and run targeted tests for every moved subsystem.
notes: Observed file sizes from review: crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/temporal.rs 9979 lines, crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs 7210 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/temporal.rs 6169 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs 4327 lines, crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/builtin_dispatch.rs 3869 lines, crates/lyng-js/gc/src/arena.rs 3142 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/bootstrap.rs 3132 lines, crates/lyng-js/objects/src/internal_methods.rs 2395 lines, crates/lyng-js/env/src/agent.rs 2234 lines, crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs 2215 lines.

Split crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs table ownership into public/metadata/core.rs, binary_data.rs, and temporal.rs while preserving the lookup facade. title: Split oversized Lyng JS core modules along ownership boundaries
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. Several core Lyng JS files are thousands of lines and mix multiple major responsibilities, contrary to the repo-level Rust module guidance. This makes ownership, review, and targeted verification harder.
acceptance: Create an incremental module split plan that preserves public APIs and crate boundaries. Split at least the highest-impact files by domain or object family without broad semantic rewrites. Keep lib.rs and main.rs thin, expose intentional public APIs via pub use, and run targeted tests for every moved subsystem.
notes: Observed file sizes from review: crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/temporal.rs 9979 lines, crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs 7210 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/temporal.rs 6169 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs 4327 lines, crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/builtin_dispatch.rs 3869 lines, crates/lyng-js/gc/src/arena.rs 3142 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/bootstrap.rs 3132 lines, crates/lyng-js/objects/src/internal_methods.rs 2395 lines, crates/lyng-js/env/src/agent.rs 2234 lines, crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs 2215 lines.

title: Split oversized Lyng JS core modules along ownership boundaries
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. Several core Lyng JS files are thousands of lines and mix multiple major responsibilities, contrary to the repo-level Rust module guidance. This makes ownership, review, and targeted verification harder.
acceptance: Create an incremental module split plan that preserves public APIs and crate boundaries. Split at least the highest-impact files by domain or object family without broad semantic rewrites. Keep lib.rs and main.rs thin, expose intentional public APIs via pub use, and run targeted tests for every moved subsystem.
notes: Observed file sizes from review: crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/temporal.rs 9979 lines, crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs 7210 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/temporal.rs 6169 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs 4327 lines, crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/builtin_dispatch.rs 3869 lines, crates/lyng-js/gc/src/arena.rs 3142 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/bootstrap.rs 3132 lines, crates/lyng-js/objects/src/internal_methods.rs 2395 lines, crates/lyng-js/env/src/agent.rs 2234 lines, crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs 2215 lines.

title: Split oversized Lyng JS core modules along ownership boundaries
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. Several core Lyng JS files are thousands of lines and mix multiple major responsibilities, contrary to the repo-level Rust module guidance. This makes ownership, review, and targeted verification harder.
acceptance: Create an incremental module split plan that preserves public APIs and crate boundaries. Split at least the highest-impact files by domain or object family without broad semantic rewrites. Keep lib.rs and main.rs thin, expose intentional public APIs via pub use, and run targeted tests for every moved subsystem.
notes: Observed file sizes from review: crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/temporal.rs 9979 lines, crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs 7210 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/temporal.rs 6169 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs 4327 lines, crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/builtin_dispatch.rs 3869 lines, crates/lyng-js/gc/src/arena.rs 3142 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/bootstrap.rs 3132 lines, crates/lyng-js/objects/src/internal_methods.rs 2395 lines, crates/lyng-js/env/src/agent.rs 2234 lines, crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs 2215 lines.

title: Split oversized Lyng JS core modules along ownership boundaries
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. Several core Lyng JS files are thousands of lines and mix multiple major responsibilities, contrary to the repo-level Rust module guidance. This makes ownership, review, and targeted verification harder.
acceptance: Create an incremental module split plan that preserves public APIs and crate boundaries. Split at least the highest-impact files by domain or object family without broad semantic rewrites. Keep lib.rs and main.rs thin, expose intentional public APIs via pub use, and run targeted tests for every moved subsystem.
notes: Observed file sizes from review: crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/temporal.rs 9979 lines, crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs 7210 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/temporal.rs 6169 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs 4327 lines, crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/builtin_dispatch.rs 3869 lines, crates/lyng-js/gc/src/arena.rs 3142 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/bootstrap.rs 3132 lines, crates/lyng-js/objects/src/internal_methods.rs 2395 lines, crates/lyng-js/env/src/agent.rs 2234 lines, crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs 2215 lines.

Split crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs table ownership into public/metadata/core.rs, binary_data.rs, and temporal.rs while preserving the lookup facade. title: Split oversized Lyng JS core modules along ownership boundaries
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. Several core Lyng JS files are thousands of lines and mix multiple major responsibilities, contrary to the repo-level Rust module guidance. This makes ownership, review, and targeted verification harder.
acceptance: Create an incremental module split plan that preserves public APIs and crate boundaries. Split at least the highest-impact files by domain or object family without broad semantic rewrites. Keep lib.rs and main.rs thin, expose intentional public APIs via pub use, and run targeted tests for every moved subsystem.
notes: Observed file sizes from review: crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/temporal.rs 9979 lines, crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs 7210 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/temporal.rs 6169 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs 4327 lines, crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/builtin_dispatch.rs 3869 lines, crates/lyng-js/gc/src/arena.rs 3142 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/bootstrap.rs 3132 lines, crates/lyng-js/objects/src/internal_methods.rs 2395 lines, crates/lyng-js/env/src/agent.rs 2234 lines, crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs 2215 lines.

comments.text: Implemented an incremental module split plan at docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-26-lyng-js-module-split.md. Split crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs table ownership into public/metadata/core.rs, binary_data.rs, and temporal.rs while preserving the lookup facade. title: Split oversized Lyng JS core modules along ownership boundaries
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. Several core Lyng JS files are thousands of lines and mix multiple major responsibilities, contrary to the repo-level Rust module guidance. This makes ownership, review, and targeted verification harder.
acceptance: Create an incremental module split plan that preserves public APIs and crate boundaries. Split at least the highest-impact files by domain or object family without broad semantic rewrites. Keep lib.rs and main.rs thin, expose intentional public APIs via pub use, and run targeted tests for every moved subsystem.
notes: Observed file sizes from review: crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/temporal.rs 9979 lines, crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs 7210 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/temporal.rs 6169 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs 4327 lines, crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/builtin_dispatch.rs 3869 lines, crates/lyng-js/gc/src/arena.rs 3142 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/bootstrap.rs 3132 lines, crates/lyng-js/objects/src/internal_methods.rs 2395 lines, crates/lyng-js/env/src/agent.rs 2234 lines, crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs 2215 lines.

comments.author: 5341456+Feryla@users.noreply.github.com
comments.text: Implemented an incremental module split plan at docs/superpowers/plans/2026-04-26-lyng-js-module-split.md. Split crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs into ids, marker, and builtin_ids modules, with builtin ID helpers separated under builtin_ids/*.rs. Split crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs table ownership into public/metadata/core.rs, binary_data.rs, and temporal.rs while preserving the lookup facade. Verification: cargo fmt --all; cargo test -p lyng-js-types (26 passed, doc-tests 0); cargo test -p lyng-js-builtins --lib (32 passed); cargo fmt --all --check.

title: Split oversized Lyng JS core modules along ownership boundaries
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. Several core Lyng JS files are thousands of lines and mix multiple major responsibilities, contrary to the repo-level Rust module guidance. This makes ownership, review, and targeted verification harder.
acceptance: Create an incremental module split plan that preserves public APIs and crate boundaries. Split at least the highest-impact files by domain or object family without broad semantic rewrites. Keep lib.rs and main.rs thin, expose intentional public APIs via pub use, and run targeted tests for every moved subsystem.
notes: Observed file sizes from review: crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/temporal.rs 9979 lines, crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs 7210 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/temporal.rs 6169 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs 4327 lines, crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/builtin_dispatch.rs 3869 lines, crates/lyng-js/gc/src/arena.rs 3142 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/bootstrap.rs 3132 lines, crates/lyng-js/objects/src/internal_methods.rs 2395 lines, crates/lyng-js/env/src/agent.rs 2234 lines, crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs 2215 lines.

title: Split oversized Lyng JS core modules along ownership boundaries
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. Several core Lyng JS files are thousands of lines and mix multiple major responsibilities, contrary to the repo-level Rust module guidance. This makes ownership, review, and targeted verification harder.
acceptance: Create an incremental module split plan that preserves public APIs and crate boundaries. Split at least the highest-impact files by domain or object family without broad semantic rewrites. Keep lib.rs and main.rs thin, expose intentional public APIs via pub use, and run targeted tests for every moved subsystem.
notes: Observed file sizes from review: crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/temporal.rs 9979 lines, crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs 7210 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/temporal.rs 6169 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs 4327 lines, crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/builtin_dispatch.rs 3869 lines, crates/lyng-js/gc/src/arena.rs 3142 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/bootstrap.rs 3132 lines, crates/lyng-js/objects/src/internal_methods.rs 2395 lines, crates/lyng-js/env/src/agent.rs 2234 lines, crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs 2215 lines.

title: Split oversized Lyng JS core modules along ownership boundaries
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. Several core Lyng JS files are thousands of lines and mix multiple major responsibilities, contrary to the repo-level Rust module guidance. This makes ownership, review, and targeted verification harder.
acceptance: Create an incremental module split plan that preserves public APIs and crate boundaries. Split at least the highest-impact files by domain or object family without broad semantic rewrites. Keep lib.rs and main.rs thin, expose intentional public APIs via pub use, and run targeted tests for every moved subsystem.
notes: Observed file sizes from review: crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/temporal.rs 9979 lines, crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs 7210 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/temporal.rs 6169 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs 4327 lines, crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/builtin_dispatch.rs 3869 lines, crates/lyng-js/gc/src/arena.rs 3142 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/bootstrap.rs 3132 lines, crates/lyng-js/objects/src/internal_methods.rs 2395 lines, crates/lyng-js/env/src/agent.rs 2234 lines, crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs 2215 lines.

title: Split oversized Lyng JS core modules along ownership boundaries
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. Several core Lyng JS files are thousands of lines and mix multiple major responsibilities, contrary to the repo-level Rust module guidance. This makes ownership, review, and targeted verification harder.
acceptance: Create an incremental module split plan that preserves public APIs and crate boundaries. Split at least the highest-impact files by domain or object family without broad semantic rewrites. Keep lib.rs and main.rs thin, expose intentional public APIs via pub use, and run targeted tests for every moved subsystem.
notes: Observed file sizes from review: crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/temporal.rs 9979 lines, crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs 7210 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/temporal.rs 6169 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs 4327 lines, crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/builtin_dispatch.rs 3869 lines, crates/lyng-js/gc/src/arena.rs 3142 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/bootstrap.rs 3132 lines, crates/lyng-js/objects/src/internal_methods.rs 2395 lines, crates/lyng-js/env/src/agent.rs 2234 lines, crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs 2215 lines.

title: Split oversized Lyng JS core modules along ownership boundaries
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. Several core Lyng JS files are thousands of lines and mix multiple major responsibilities, contrary to the repo-level Rust module guidance. This makes ownership, review, and targeted verification harder.
acceptance: Create an incremental module split plan that preserves public APIs and crate boundaries. Split at least the highest-impact files by domain or object family without broad semantic rewrites. Keep lib.rs and main.rs thin, expose intentional public APIs via pub use, and run targeted tests for every moved subsystem.
notes: Observed file sizes from review: crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/temporal.rs 9979 lines, crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs 7210 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/temporal.rs 6169 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs 4327 lines, crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/builtin_dispatch.rs 3869 lines, crates/lyng-js/gc/src/arena.rs 3142 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/bootstrap.rs 3132 lines, crates/lyng-js/objects/src/internal_methods.rs 2395 lines, crates/lyng-js/env/src/agent.rs 2234 lines, crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs 2215 lines.

title: Split oversized Lyng JS core modules along ownership boundaries
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. Several core Lyng JS files are thousands of lines and mix multiple major responsibilities, contrary to the repo-level Rust module guidance. This makes ownership, review, and targeted verification harder.
acceptance: Create an incremental module split plan that preserves public APIs and crate boundaries. Split at least the highest-impact files by domain or object family without broad semantic rewrites. Keep lib.rs and main.rs thin, expose intentional public APIs via pub use, and run targeted tests for every moved subsystem.
notes: Observed file sizes from review: crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/temporal.rs 9979 lines, crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs 7210 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/temporal.rs 6169 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs 4327 lines, crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/builtin_dispatch.rs 3869 lines, crates/lyng-js/gc/src/arena.rs 3142 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/bootstrap.rs 3132 lines, crates/lyng-js/objects/src/internal_methods.rs 2395 lines, crates/lyng-js/env/src/agent.rs 2234 lines, crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs 2215 lines.

title: Split oversized Lyng JS core modules along ownership boundaries
description: Finding from April 25 2026 Lyng JS code review. Several core Lyng JS files are thousands of lines and mix multiple major responsibilities, contrary to the repo-level Rust module guidance. This makes ownership, review, and targeted verification harder.
acceptance: Create an incremental module split plan that preserves public APIs and crate boundaries. Split at least the highest-impact files by domain or object family without broad semantic rewrites. Keep lib.rs and main.rs thin, expose intentional public APIs via pub use, and run targeted tests for every moved subsystem.
notes: Observed file sizes from review: crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/dispatch/temporal.rs 9979 lines, crates/lyng-js/types/src/lib.rs 7210 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/temporal.rs 6169 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/public/metadata.rs 4327 lines, crates/lyng-js/vm/src/vm/builtin_dispatch.rs 3869 lines, crates/lyng-js/gc/src/arena.rs 3142 lines, crates/lyng-js/builtins/src/bootstrap.rs 3132 lines, crates/lyng-js/objects/src/internal_methods.rs 2395 lines, crates/lyng-js/env/src/agent.rs 2234 lines, crates/lyng-js/ops/src/object.rs 2215 lines.
